text: R interface FFmpeg MediaInfo reproducible media preprocessing research/data-science pipelines — batch trimming, cropping, format standardization, metadata extraction tibbles. deliberately “ffmpeg R” (see project/DECISIONS.md D001).

title: tidymedia
text: Layer 0 — escape hatch: ffmpeg(), ffprobe(), mediainfo() pass raw arguments CLIs. “faithful wrapper” . Layer 1 — engine: ffm_* pipe builder (R/ffm.R, R/ffm_oop.R). command assembly, quoting, copy-vs-re-encode logic lives , . Deliberately linear: one input chain, sequential filters, one output, plus blessed multi-input verbs (stack/concat/overlay). filtergraph DAGs. Layer 2 — front door: task verbs (extract_audio(), segment_video(), …) thin wrappers Layer 1. Must glue command strings.

title: Contributing to tidymedia
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("jmgirard/tidymedia", fork = TRUE). Install development dependences devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). title: Batch processing
text: ffm_batch() general-purpose engine: takes jobs tibble (one row per output) function turns row pipeline. column passed function name, purrr::pmap()-style, runner returns jobs tibble command column added (success column run = TRUE). build pipeline , combination builder verbs available per job. Give .f ... argument tolerates extra job columns use.
code: jobs <- tibble::tibble(   input  = c(video, video),   output = c("clip1.mp3", "clip2.mp3") )  ffm_batch(jobs, run = FALSE, .f = function(input, output, ...) {   ffm_files(input, output) |>     ffm_drop("video") |>     ffm_codec(audio = "libmp3lame") }) #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   input                                                        output    command #>   <chr>                                                        <chr>     <chr>   #> 1 /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/tidymedia/extdata/sample.mp4 clip1.mp3 "-y -i… #> 2 /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/tidymedia/extdata/sample.mp4 clip2.mp3 "-y -i…

title: Batch processing
text: tasks turn one input many outputs. fan-verbs Layer 2 wrappers built ffm_batch(). segment_video() cuts file pieces given start/stop timestamps, returning one row per segment: separate_audio_video() splits file audio video streams, returning two compiled commands:
code: segment_video(   video,   ts_start = c(0, 0.5),   ts_stop  = c(0.5, 1),   run = FALSE ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 5 #>   input                                               output start   end command #>   <chr>                                               <chr>  <dbl> <dbl> <chr>   #> 1 /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/tidymedia/extdata/… /home…   0     0.5 "-y -i… #> 2 /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/tidymedia/extdata/… /home…   0.5   1   "-y -i… separate_audio_video(video, "audio.aac", "video.mp4", run = FALSE) #>                                                                                           audio  #> "-y -i \"/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/tidymedia/extdata/sample.mp4\" -map 0:a \"audio.aac\""  #>                                                                                           video  #> "-y -i \"/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/tidymedia/extdata/sample.mp4\" -map 0:v \"video.mp4\""

title: Batch processing
text: ffm_batch() fan-verbs accept parallel = TRUE, maps jobs furrr. Parallelism follows whatever future plan set, opt explicitly: Every job carries compiled command returned tibble, batch run fully reproducible: save column exact record FFmpeg commands produced outputs.
code: library(future) plan(multisession)  ffm_batch(jobs, parallel = TRUE, .f = function(input, output, ...) {   ffm_files(input, output) |> ffm_drop("video") |> ffm_codec(audio = "libmp3lame") })

title: Media metadata as tibbles
text: MediaInfo organizes metadata sections (General, Video, Audio, …). mediainfo_query() pulls several parameters one section tibble: mediainfo_template() applies whole template ; two ship package ("brief" "extended"): single values, get_*() helpers quickest path:
code: mediainfo_query(   video,   section = "Video",   parameters = c("Width", "Height", "FrameRate") ) #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   file                                                    Width Height FrameRate #>   <chr>                                                   <int>  <int>     <dbl> #> 1 /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/tidymedia/extdata/samp…   320    240        15 mediainfo_template(video, template = "brief") #> # A tibble: 1 × 12 #>   file           complete_name format file_size duration width height frame_rate #>   <chr>          <chr>         <chr>      <int>    <int> <int>  <int>      <dbl> #> 1 /home/runner/… /home/runner… MPEG-4     17725     1000   320    240         15 #> # ℹ 4 more variables: video_bit_rate <int>, channels <int>, #> #   sampling_rate <int>, audio_bit_rate <int> get_duration(video, unit = "sec") #> [1] 1 get_width(video) #> [1] 320 get_height(video) #> [1] 240

title: Get started with tidymedia
text: tidymedia organized three layers, lowest highest level: Layer 0 — escape hatch. ffmpeg(), ffprobe(), mediainfo() pass raw argument string straight corresponding command-line tool. Use need something tidymedia wrap. Layer 1 — pipeline builder. ffm_* functions assemble FFmpeg command step step. quoting, option ordering, copy-vs-re-encode logic lives . Layer 2 — task verbs. Functions like extract_audio() segment_video() thin wrappers builder common jobs. See vignette("batch") processing many files . vignette focuses Layer 1, builder, heart package.

title: Get started with tidymedia
text: Every pipeline starts ffm() (alias ffm_files()), names input output files. Throughout vignette use tiny sample clip ships package: add steps |>. ffm_* verb records instruction; nothing runs ask . Calling ffm_compile() turns pipeline exact FFmpeg command represents — reproducible string can inspect, log, run: Printing pipeline shows compiled command, can eyeball pipeline point: ready, swap ffm_compile() ffm_run() execute command FFmpeg write output file.
code: video <- system.file("extdata", "sample.mp4", package = "tidymedia") ffm(video, "output.mp4") |>   ffm_trim(start = 1, end = 5) |>   ffm_crop(width = 160, height = 120) |>   ffm_codec(video = "libx264") |>   ffm_compile() #> [1] "-y -i \"/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/tidymedia/extdata/sample.mp4\" -vf \"trim=start=1:end=5,setpts=PTS-STARTPTS,crop=w=160:h=120:x=(in_w-out_w)/2:y=(in_h-out_h)/2\" -codec:v libx264 \"output.mp4\"" ffm(video, "output.mp4") |>   ffm_scale(width = 320, height = 240) |>   ffm_pixel_format("yuv420p") #> tidymedia ffmpeg pipeline: #>  #>  -y -i "/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/tidymedia/extdata/sample.mp4" -vf "scale=w=320:h=240" -pix_fmt yuv420p "output.mp4"

title: Get started with tidymedia
text: Cutting clip can frame-accurate (re-encoded, slower) fast lossless (stream copy snaps keyframes). ffm_seek() exposes via reencode argument, pairs naturally ffm_copy() fast path: Note ffm_seek() (uses FFmpeg’s -ss/-options) distinct ffm_trim() (trim filter): seeking can stream-copy.
code: # Fast, lossless cut ffm(video, "output.mp4") |>   ffm_seek(start = 1, end = 5, reencode = FALSE) |>   ffm_copy() |>   ffm_compile() #> [1] "-y -ss 1 -to 5 -i \"/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/tidymedia/extdata/sample.mp4\" -codec:v copy -codec:a copy -avoid_negative_ts make_zero -map 0 \"output.mp4\""

title: Get started with tidymedia
text: “blessed” verbs take one input. Pass vector files ffm(), stack side side ffm_hstack() join end--end ffm_concat(): builder stays deliberately linear — one input chain, sequential filters, one output — full FFmpeg filtergraphs scope. need one, reach Layer 0 escape hatch:
code: ffm(c(video, video), "side_by_side.mp4") |>   ffm_hstack() |>   ffm_compile() #> [1] "-y -i \"/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/tidymedia/extdata/sample.mp4\" -i \"/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/tidymedia/extdata/sample.mp4\" -filter_complex \"[0:v][1:v]hstack=inputs=2:shortest=0[vout]\" -map \"[vout]\" \"side_by_side.mp4\"" # Layer 0: raw arguments passed straight to FFmpeg ffmpeg("-version")[1] #> [1] "ffmpeg version 6.1.1-3ubuntu5 Copyright (c) 2000-2023 the FFmpeg developers"

text: infile string containing path video file. ts_start vector containing one timestamps indicating start segment create. Can either numeric vector indicating seconds character vector time duration syntax. Must length ts_stop. ts_stop vector containing one timestamps indicating stop segment create. title: Tidy eval helpers — tidyeval
text: page lists tidy eval tools reexported package rlang. learn using tidy eval scripts packages high level, see dplyr programming vignette ggplot2 packages vignette. Metaprogramming section Advanced R may also useful deeper dive. tidy eval operators {{, !!, !!! syntactic constructs specially interpreted tidy eval functions. mostly need {{, !! !!! advanced operators use simple cases. curly-curly operator {{ allows tunnel data-variables passed function arguments inside tidy eval functions. {{ designed individual arguments. pass multiple arguments contained dots, use ... normal way.   enquo() enquos() delay execution one several function arguments. former returns single expression, latter returns list expressions. defused, expressions longer evaluate . must injected back evaluation context !! (single expression) !!! (list expressions).   simple case, code equivalent usage {{ ... . Defusing enquo() enquos() needed complex cases, instance need inspect modify expressions way. .data pronoun object represents current slice data. variable name string, use .data pronoun subset variable [[.   Another tidy eval operator :=. makes possible use glue curly-curly syntax LHS =. technical reasons, R language support complex expressions left =, use := workaround.   Many tidy eval functions like dplyr::mutate() dplyr::summarise() give automatic name unnamed inputs. need create sort automatic names , use as_label(). instance, glue-tunnelling syntax can reproduced manually :   Expressions defused enquo() (tunnelled {{) need simple column names, can arbitrarily complex. as_label() handles cases gracefully. code assumes simple column name, use as_name() instead. safer throws error input name expected.
code: my_function <- function(data, var, ...) {   data %>%     group_by(...) %>%     summarise(mean = mean({{ var }})) } my_function <- function(data, var, ...) {   # Defuse   var <- enquo(var)   dots <- enquos(...)    # Inject   data %>%     group_by(!!!dots) %>%     summarise(mean = mean(!!var)) } my_var <- "disp" mtcars %>% summarise(mean = mean(.data[[my_var]])) my_function <- function(data, var, suffix = "foo") {   # Use `{{` to tunnel function arguments and the usual glue   # operator `{` to interpolate plain strings.   data %>%     summarise("{{ var }}_mean_{suffix}" := mean({{ var }})) } my_function <- function(data, var, suffix = "foo") {   var <- enquo(var)   prefix <- as_label(var)   data %>%     summarise("{prefix}_mean_{suffix}" := mean(!!var)) }

title: tidymedia 0.1.0
text: First tagged release, bringing metadata, builder, task-verb work 0.0.0.900x development series documented, release-ready state.

title: tidymedia 0.1.0
text: Every exported function now carries worked example architecture-layer @family tag, pkgdown site whose reference index grouped three layers (escape hatch, builder, task verbs). Added three vignettes: Get started (building pipelines, vignette("tidymedia")), Media metadata tibbles (vignette("metadata")), Batch processing (vignette("batch")). small sample clip now ships inst/extdata/sample.mp4 examples vignettes runnable.

title: tidymedia 0.1.0
text: MediaInfo FFprobe readers now accept vector files return one stacked tibble keyed leading file column, metadata whole batch ready dplyr joins filters. covers probe_all(), probe_*() shortcuts, mediainfo_query(), mediainfo_template(), mediainfo_parameter(), get_*() convenience helpers. Typed output now default. Every reader gains typed argument (default TRUE) converts numeric columns integers/doubles turns missing markers (FFprobe’s "N/", MediaInfo’s empty values) NA; fractions, ratios, hex identifiers, text stay strings. Pass typed = FALSE previous -character behavior. replaces probe_all()’s convert argument. Readers resilient unreadable files: missing file, one probed, among several yields -NA row (NA value) plus warning, instead aborting whole call. Malformed arguments still abort. Arguments now passed CLIs argument vectors (system2()) rather interpolated shell string, file paths MediaInfo --Inform templates containing spaces, quotes, ;, %, $ work correctly. Layer 0 escape hatches mediainfo() / ffprobe() keep raw-string signatures. Output column schemas unified: readers lead file column two built-MediaInfo templates now emit snake_case column names. User-supplied names (mediainfo_query(names =), custom template headers) kept verbatim.

title: tidymedia 0.1.0
text: ffmpeg() longer lets FFmpeg read calling process’s standard input, running pipeline (e.g. via ffm_run()) inside script fed stdin longer swallows rest input. Equivalent FFmpeg’s -nostdin. probe_container(), probe_streams(), probe_video(), probe_audio() now return requested tibble called infile =; previously returned NULL. convert_fractions() parses fractions directly instead via eval(parse()), passes NA , errors values neither number fraction. FFprobe’s key=value output split first = , values contain = longer truncated; superseded tidyr::separate() call gone. Files zero streams longer trip stream loop.

title: tidymedia 0.0.0.9002
text: Added ffm_batch(), tidymedia batch entry point: maps pipeline-building function every row jobs data frame (columns passed function name, purrr::pmap()-style), compiles one reproducible command per job, optionally runs . returns jobs tibble added command column , run, success column. Set parallel = TRUE map furrr following active future plan.

what: Task verbs rebuilt on the builder
title: tidymedia 0.0.0.9002
text: Every task verb now thin wrapper Layer 1 ffm_* builder longer assembles FFmpeg command string. gains run argument returns compiled, reproducible command (invisibly run): extract_audio(), audio_as_mp3(), crop_video(), format_for_web(), extract_frame(), separate_audio_video(), segment_video(), concatenate_videos(). segment_video() now built ffm_batch(): returns job tibble (one row per segment command run status) gains reencode parallel arguments. Cutting frame-accurate default. segment_video() new ffm_seek() default reencode = TRUE, re-encodes cuts land exact requested frames. reencode = FALSE selects fast, lossless copy snaps nearest keyframes (output duration may differ one group--pictures). previous copy-based segment_video() cut wrong point shifted timestamps; behavior gone. Breaking: extract_audio()’s free-form options string replaced acodec argument; crop_video() drops arg argument now centers crop default; separate_audio_video() returns named vector two commands; segment_video() returns tibble.

title: tidymedia 0.0.0.9002
text: Added ffm_seek(), seek-based cut using -ss/-(distinct trim filter ffm_trim()), cuts can stream-copy. Accurate seeks output-seek re-encode; fast copy seeks input-seek add -avoid_negative_ts. Added ffm_concat(), blessed multi-input verb concatenates pipeline’s inputs via FFmpeg’s concat demuxer (fast, lossless, -format). Added ffm_output_options(), controlled passthrough raw output options ffm_compile() still positions quotes.

title: tidymedia 0.0.0.9001
text: Reworked Layer 1 ffm_* builder onto structured command model: ffm_compile() now single place assembles, positions, quotes every option. Single-input filter chains compile -vf/-af; multi-input stacking compiles valid -filter_complex graph explicit stream labels automatic -map. Fixed four builder bugs: ffm_trim(setpts = FALSE) longer forces setpts filter; ffm_drop() flags now output options placed input (-); ffm_pixel_format() longer runs output filename; previously invalid -filter_complex:v output gone. ffm_compile() now errors early stream set codec copy filter targets stream, instead failing cryptically ffmpeg. ffm_hstack() must applied video filters now produces runnable command (verified end--end ffmpeg).

title: tidymedia 0.0.0.9001
text: Added testthat (3rd edition) test suite covering ffm_* pipeline builder ffm_compile() output, plus binary-gated tests ffmpeg/ffprobe/mediainfo task functions. Added GitHub Actions workflows R CMD check (macOS, Windows, Linux) test coverage; Linux jobs install ffmpeg mediainfo execution tests run CI. input validation user-facing messages now use rlang’s check_* helpers cli (cli::cli_abort() / cli::cli_warn()); assertthat dependency removed. Added dplyr, tidyr, purrr, cli Imports (first three already used undeclared). Enumerated arguments (e.g. units, unit, section, template, program) now matched exactly via rlang::arg_match() instead partial matching match.arg(); pass full value.

title: tidymedia 0.0.0.9001
text: mediainfo_parameter() (helpers built : get_duration(), get_framerate(), get_width(), get_height(), get_samplingrate()) now shell-quote --Inform argument, work POSIX shells ; previously parsed command separator.
